channel.title: Cost Nimbus
channel.description: Cloud cost intelligence built by engineers, for engineers. Real numbers, no vendor fluff.

item.title: Agentic SOC: Hype or Real Shift?
item.description: A SOC infrastructure builder's honest take on agentic AI in security operations — what it actually means, where the marketing ends, and a 3-question evaluation framework for cutting through vendor noise.

item.title: The Agentic SOC: What Platform Builders Actually Need to Know
item.description: Every vendor at RSA 2026 is selling 'agentic AI.' This is for the engineers building the platform those agents run on — unified data layers, multi-agent orchestration, audit trails, guardrails, and the deduplication problem nobody talks about.

item.title: AI Agent Governance for SOC Teams: From Copilot to Autonomous
item.description: The gap between 'AI helps analysts' and 'AI acts autonomously' is where governance frameworks live or die. Here's the risk matrix, approval workflows, and audit requirements SOC teams need as they move up the autonomy spectrum.

item.title: RSAC 2026: What the Agentic SOC Announcements Actually Mean for Your Budget
item.description: Every vendor at RSAC 2026 is pitching an agentic SOC. Here's what 5 simultaneous AI security agent subscriptions actually cost — and how to avoid paying for the same capability three times.

item.title: AI Security Risks Enterprises Are Ignoring
item.description: Only 34% of organizations know where all their data resides, yet AI agents are wandering through enterprise systems with unprecedented access. Here are the four AI security risks that aren't in your threat model yet — and should be.

item.title: AI SOC Investigation Has Moved Beyond Triage — And Most Platforms Aren't Ready
item.description: The industry fixated on using AI to close tickets faster. The real leverage is in investigation — correlating multi-source signals to catch what triage-only systems miss. Here's what that actually looks like in production.

item.title: The $50K Question: Which SOC Platform Actually Fits Your Team?
item.description: A 2026 buyer's guide to SOC tools from someone who builds the platforms — covering SIEM, SOAR, AI-native tools, and the real costs nobody talks about.

item.title: OPA v1.0 Migration Guide for SOC Teams
item.description: OPA's v1.0 release brings breaking changes to Rego syntax and defaults. Here's a practical migration guide for SOC and infrastructure security teams — covering what changed, what breaks, and how to use the migration as an opportunity to improve your compliance posture.

item.title: Security Alert Fatigue Is Killing Your SOC — Here's What Actually Works
item.description: A SOC infrastructure engineer's honest analysis of alert fatigue in the security operations center: the real numbers, why current tools fail, and the practical approaches that actually reduce noise without creating blind spots.

item.title: Autonomous FinOps: From Reactive Alerts to Self-Driving Cloud Optimization
item.description: Most teams are stuck on the FinOps treadmill — reactive cleanup, temporary improvement, inevitable drift. Here's the architecture for breaking that cycle: guardrails, drift detection, and what Level 3 autonomous optimization actually requires to work safely.

item.title: Google + Wiz: What the $32B Acquisition Means for Your Cloud Security Spend
item.description: Google closed the $32B Wiz acquisition on March 11 — days before RSAC. Here's what it means for customers with existing Wiz contracts, multi-cloud buyers evaluating CNAPP, and anyone watching cloud security consolidation.

item.title: The SOC Analyst's Guide to Generative AI
item.description: A practical guide for security analysts on safely integrating generative AI into daily workflows — from SIEM query generation to threat intel summarization. What actually works, what doesn't, and how to avoid the traps.

item.title: Azure vs AWS for .NET Shops: A Cost and DX Deep-Dive
item.description: You can run .NET on AWS. But Azure is purpose-built for it — and in most cases, dramatically cheaper. A rigorous comparison of managed services, pricing, and DX for teams migrating from on-prem or choosing a cloud for their .NET stack.

item.title: GPU Cost Management for ML Teams: A Practical Playbook
item.description: AWS cut H100 prices 44% in 2025. GCP followed. If your ML team set its GPU strategy before mid-2025, you're operating on stale assumptions. Here's the current pricing reality and the three highest-leverage cost levers available right now.

item.title: Policy-as-Code for SOC Teams: Stop Writing Tickets, Start Writing Rules
item.description: How SOC infrastructure engineers can use OPA and Rego policies to automate security enforcement, eliminate retroactive approval theater, and build continuous SOC2 compliance — with real code from a production policy library.

item.title: Static vs Dynamic Playbooks: Why 2026 Is the Year of AI-Generated IR
item.description: IDC predicts 85% of detection and response playbooks will be dynamically generated by H1 2027. Here's what that means for your SOC, why static playbooks are already failing, and how to prepare your team for the shift.

item.title: GPU Cost Optimization: When AI Ambitions Meet Budget Reality
item.description: A practical guide to cutting GPU costs for AI/ML workloads — where the waste actually hides, spot instance strategies, model optimization, and real cost breakdowns for A100, H100, and L4 instances.

item.title: GPU FinOps: Why Traditional Cloud Cost Tools Fail for AI Workloads
item.description: Your FinOps dashboard was built for CPU compute and storage. It doesn't understand GPU utilization, spot scheduling for training jobs, or the economics of reserved capacity for inference vs training. Here's what's missing and how to close the gap.

item.title: Microsoft Sentinel's New Pricing Model: What Changed in March 2026 and What It Costs
item.description: Microsoft shifted Sentinel Accelerator from daily to monthly usage pricing on March 1, 2026. Here's exactly what changed, who it affects, and how to model your new TCO before your next contract review.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Manual Playbook Maintenance
item.description: No one budgets for playbook rot, but every SOC pays for it. A look at the real resource drain of manual IR documentation — and what it costs when your procedures are wrong at the moment you need them most.

item.title: How Much Does SOC 2 Cost in 2026? Audit, Certification & Hidden Fees
item.description: A brutally honest SOC 2 cost breakdown covering auditor fees, soc 2 certification cost, tool costs, internal labor hours, and the hidden opportunity cost nobody talks about — with real numbers by company size.

item.title: The Complete SOC 2 Compliance Checklist: Evidence for Your SOC 2 Audit
item.description: The practical SOC 2 compliance checklist for evidence collection — organized by Trust Service Criteria, covering all SOC 2 requirements auditors expect, with sources, frequency, and manual vs automated time estimates.

item.title: How to Cut Your AWS Egress Bill by 80% (Without Changing Your App)
item.description: AWS charges $0.09/GB to move data out. Here are five proven techniques—VPC endpoints, Cloudflare R2, CloudFront, PrivateLink, and S3 Transfer Acceleration avoidance—that teams use to cut egress by 70–91%.

item.title: Kubernetes Cost Optimization: How We Cut Our K8s Bill by 67%
item.description: Step-by-step guide to cutting Kubernetes costs: right-sizing pods, Spot/Preemptible nodes, Cluster Autoscaler tuning, namespace quotas, and the open-source tools that do the heavy lifting.

item.title: Lambda Cold Starts: The Hidden Tax on Your Serverless Bill
item.description: The uncomfortable truth about Lambda cold start optimization — fixing them often costs more than the problem itself. Real math on provisioned concurrency, SnapStart, keep-warm strategies, and when to skip Lambda entirely.

item.title: Lambda Cold Starts: The Complete 2026 Optimization Guide
item.description: Lambda cold starts can add 1–8 seconds to your response time. Here are 9 proven techniques to eliminate them — from Provisioned Concurrency to SnapStart to runtime selection.

item.title: RDS vs Aurora: The Real Cost Difference in 2026
item.description: Aurora can cost 3× more than RDS at small scale, or save you money at high I/O. Here's the complete decision framework with real pricing numbers.

item.title: AWS Savings Plans vs Reserved Instances: Which Saves More in 2026?
item.description: Savings Plans are more flexible, but Reserved Instances can save 5–10% more in specific scenarios. Here is the complete decision guide with real numbers.

item.title: The Hidden Costs of AWS NAT Gateways (And How to Cut Them by 80%)
item.description: I saved $18,000 per month by optimizing NAT Gateway usage. Learn how VPC endpoints, NAT instances, and IPv6 can slash your AWS networking costs.

item.title: How I Saved $50K/Month in Cloud Costs
item.description: Two specific solutions that delivered $30K + $20K in monthly savings, with exact technologies and step-by-step implementation.

item.title: Non-Human Identity Security: The $10M Risk Nobody's Managing
item.description: Service accounts outnumber humans 45:1. Most rotate credentials never. GitGuardian found 12.8 million secrets leaked on GitHub in 2023 alone. The math on NHI compromise is brutal — and most organizations have no governance program for it.
